title: Pediatric Anemia Evidence Document
description: Validates modules/anemia/evidence.json: the single evidence source (DEF-1) — a knowledge-base-version-stamped list of cited sources, each carrying its existing prose (`supports`) and, per EP-3, an array of exact-passage-level `passages[]` records. Structural validity never implies clinical validity: a passage validating here only means the record is shaped correctly, not that its content is accurate or locally applicable.

doi.description: Optional. Not every source has a DOI (e.g. WHO2024_HB, a WHO guideline, does not); this property is simply absent rather than null on those records, matching every other genuinely-optional descriptive field on this object (journal-style citation metadata, not a passage-level clinical fact) — see `$defs/passage` for the fields where the codebase's typed-null convention instead applies.

pmcid.description: Optional; present only when the source has a PubMed Central id.

recencyNote.description: Optional free-text note explaining why an older source is still retained.

passages.description: Exact-passage-level evidence records for this source (EP-3, D-EP3-2/D-EP3-3). Every source has at least one: the minted `<id>#implementation-proposal` sentinel, plus one per source-supported passage backfilled from the RF-EV-001 bundle.

access_basis.description: EPR2-T1 (FR-WP2-01/05). REQUIRED — omission fails validation; an unassessed source states that explicitly as `unknown` rather than omitting the key (D2). How this repo actually accesses this source's content today. Vocabulary matches schemas/rights/rights_record.schema.json's `access.basis` one-for-one, including the `unknown` local amendment (EPR0-T3, handoff §9.3) so this field and the source's joined rights_record (rights/rights-ledger.json, D4) never disagree on a name for the same concept. This is a lightweight, always-present source-level summary a consumer can read without resolving the ledger — it is NOT a duplicate of the full rights_record and carries no clearance authority itself (D6/D7): a coverage gate may read it, a clearance gate may not exist to read it.

license.description: EPR2-T1 (FR-WP2-01/05). REQUIRED — omission fails validation; every member below is itself required with an explicit typed unknown/null where genuinely unassessed. Source-level copyright/licence posture, a lightweight sibling of schemas/rights/rights_record.schema.json's `copyright` object — the authoritative, extensible governance record lives in rights/, joined via rights/rights-ledger.json (D4); this field is not a duplicate of it and carries no clearance authority (D6/D7).

terms.description: EPR2-T1 (FR-WP2-01/03/05). REQUIRED — omission fails validation; every member below is itself required with an explicit typed unknown where genuinely unassessed. Source-level terms-of-use restrictions transcribed from an observed terms document, a lightweight sibling of schemas/rights/rights_record.schema.json's `contract` object — the authoritative record lives in rights/, joined via rights/rights-ledger.json (D4). `terms_snapshot` (EPR2-T2) separately records the locator and date the terms were observed at; this object records the restriction facts transcribed from that locator, never the terms prose itself (D1) — a fixture attempting to store terms text in any member here is rejected by the closed enums below, none of which is a free-text type.

license.description: EPR2-T1 (FR-WP2-01/05). Source-level copyright/licence posture. Every member is required; use the typed unknown/null member rather than omitting the key when genuinely unassessed (D2). Field names and enum vocabulary are drawn one-for-one from schemas/rights/rights_record.schema.json's `copyright` object so this lightweight summary and the source's joined rights_record (rights/rights-ledger.json, D4) never disagree on a name for the same concept — this object is not a duplicate of the full rights_record and carries no clearance authority of its own.

status.description: Matches schemas/rights/rights_record.schema.json's `copyright.status` enum exactly. `us_federal_government_work` is a distinct member from `public_domain` by design — it names the 17 U.S.C. §105 basis, never a public-domain claim resting on funding source alone (see EPR2-T4, FR-WP2-04, which extends this object to record that distinction explicitly).

rights_holder.description: The named copyright holder, or null when unassessed or genuinely unstated by the source.

license_url.description: No `format: "uri"` (FR-WP0-08) — scripts/lib/json-schema-lite.mjs silently ignores `format` except date/date-time, so an unenforced `format: "uri"` would fail open. Null when no published licence URL is known or the field is unassessed; a non-null value that is not a URL is rejected by `pattern`.

noncommercial_only.description: Null when unassessed. True/false only once actually determined from the licence/terms.

no_derivatives.description: Null when unassessed. True/false only once actually determined from the licence/terms.

terms.description: EPR2-T1 (FR-WP2-01/03/05). Source-level terms-of-use restrictions. Every member is required; use the closed enum's `unknown` (or `not_addressed`) member rather than omitting the key when genuinely unassessed (D2). Field names and enum vocabulary are drawn one-for-one from schemas/rights/rights_record.schema.json's `contract` object — this object is not a duplicate of the full rights_record and carries no clearance authority of its own; the authoritative record lives in rights/, joined via rights/rights-ledger.json (D4). Every member is a closed enum: there is no free-text member anywhere on this object capable of holding terms prose (D1) — the facts recorded here must be transcribed from an observed terms document (see `terms_snapshot`, EPR2-T2), never authored.

adaptation.description: Matches schemas/rights/rights_record.schema.json's `contract.adaptation` enum exactly — covers altering, abridging, adapting, or modifying the source content, or preparing a derivative work based on it.

commercial_use.description: A local extension of schemas/rights/rights_record.schema.json's `contract.commercial_use` enum, which closes with `not_granted`. This field adds `not_granted_by_subscription` — the literal value the vendored spec bundle's own prose narrative uses for the AAP2026_IDA case (Research_Foundry_Source_Reuse_and_Rights_Governance_Spec_v1.0.md, Appendix A; FR-WP2-03): a subscription that grants read access grants no commercial-use right, a more precise claim than the generic `not_granted`. This field lives on schemas/evidence.schema.json, not on the vendored schemas/rights/rights_record.schema.json, so extending its enum here is not a vendored-schema amendment and needs no schemas/rights/VENDORING.md entry.

passage.description: A single exact-passage evidence record (D-EP3-3). Every member below except `applicability` is REQUIRED as a key — legitimate absence is expressed as an explicit typed null (`evidenceGrade`, `supersedes`, and every member of `sourceLocator`/`applicability`), never by omitting the key. `applicability` is conditionally required by the allOf below (AC-WP3-RESIL, EP3-T6): required on every `source-supported` record (an unrestricted-by-default clinical applicability claim on a supposedly source-backed record is a safety defect), optional only on the `implementation-proposal` sentinel, where absence means "unrestricted" because there is no clinical claim being made at all. The one content invariant (AC EP3-T1) is enforced by the first allOf member below: a record is valid only when `exactPassage` is non-empty OR `status` is `implementation-proposal` — the proposal sentinel is the only way to have an empty passage.

id.description: "<sourceId>#<rf evidence_id>", or "<sourceId>#implementation-proposal" for the minted sentinel.

sourceId.description: Must equal the `id` of the enclosing source record. (This schema validates the passage in isolation and cannot cross-check the parent; that equality is enforced by scripts/evidence/build-evidence-pack.mjs and tests/evidence-passages.test.mjs.)

status.description: `source-supported` means located AND carrying zero `reviewFlags` (reviewer-gate fix-2). `quarantined` means a passage was located, but the independent EP3-T5 fidelity audit found a defect (a non-empty `reviewFlags`) that prevents it from grounding a clinical claim — it is a defective source claim, not an implementation proposal, and the two must not be conflated: a proposal never claimed a located passage, a quarantined record did and that claim did not hold up. `implementation-proposal` is the explicit minted sentinel (D-EP3-3) — exactly one per source.

exactPassage.description: A PARAPHRASE (see `passageFidelity`, D-EP3-4) of the located passage, sourced from the RF extracted point's `summary` — never its `quote`. Empty string only on an `implementation-proposal` record.

passageFidelity.description: States what kind of text `exactPassage` holds. REG-002 has not cleared verbatim reuse of guideline text, so every record minted by this codebase's converter is `paraphrase` unless withheld; `verbatim` is schema-representable for a future cleared source but is asserted by nobody today. `withheld` (EP3T5-F01) means the independent EP3-T5 fidelity audit found the located span shared a rights-restricted contiguous span with source text; vendor-rf-bundle.mjs replaced `exactPassage` with the fixed placeholder before this record was ever minted, and `reviewFlags` carries `near-verbatim-span-pending-rights`.

reviewFlags.description: EP3-T5: zero or more independent-audit fidelity flags naming a specific way this passage over- or under-claims relative to its located source passage. Closed enum matching evidence-packs/rf-ev-001/fidelity-findings.json's `flagSemantics` keys — nothing outside that file may add a flag here. Empty array on a clean record. ANY non-empty value here means `status` MUST be `quarantined` (reviewer-gate fix-2, enforced by the allOf below) and blocks source-supported binding — see src/evidence.js#isBindableAsSourceSupported, the single shared predicate EP-4's rule->passage binder and scripts/validate-kb.mjs both call. Flags only suppress a claim; they never add or strengthen one.

reviewFindingIds.description: EP3-T5: the evidence-packs/rf-ev-001/fidelity-findings.json finding id(s) (e.g. "EP3T5-F02") that produced this record's `reviewFlags`. Empty array on a clean record. Every entry must resolve to a real finding id, and the flags it names must exactly match `reviewFlags`; both directions are enforced cross-file by scripts/validate-kb.mjs, which this schema cannot reach into the findings file to check on its own.

evidenceGrade.description: Null on an `implementation-proposal` record (there is no grade to assign to a sentinel). Otherwise the mapped `pediatric_cds.classification` from the RF extracted point (e.g. `source_supported_fact` -> `source-supported-fact`).

supersedes.description: Free-text description of what this passage's guidance supersedes, from `pediatric_cds.lifecycle.supersedes`. Null when the source states no supersession.

surveillanceQuery.description: A standing search query for detecting whether this source has been updated. Asserted per source (not derivable from any single RF extracted point) by the converter's source map.

allOf.description: AC-WP3-RESIL (EP3-T6): applicability is required unless the record IS the implementation-proposal sentinel.
EP3-T5 (EP3T5-F01): a record flagged near-verbatim-span-pending-rights must be marked withheld.
EP3-T5 (EP3T5-F01): the converse — a record marked withheld must carry the flag that caused it.
Reviewer-gate fix-2: `source-supported` now strictly means located AND unflagged — a record cannot declare itself source-supported while carrying an audit flag.
Reviewer-gate fix-2: the converse — any non-empty reviewFlags forces status to quarantined, so a flagged record can never still read as source-supported or as an implementation-proposal.

Reviewer-gate re-review fix (finding B). SCOPE, stated precisely (corrected in the fourth pass, which flagged the earlier wording as overstated): the equivalence holds only over LOCATED records — source-supported means empty reviewFlags, and quarantined means non-empty reviewFlags. It is NOT a global property of every passage: the six implementation-proposal sentinels legitimately carry empty reviewFlags without being source-supported, because they assert no located passage at all. This clause is the quarantined-implies-non-empty half. Without this, {status: "quarantined", reviewFlags: []} validated — a record could claim the defect-flag status while carrying none of the flags that justify it. `status: "quarantined"` now requires at least one reviewFlags entry, so `quarantined` and `reviewFlags: []` can never coexist (mirrors the source-supported=>empty-flags rule above, in the opposite direction).

sourceLocator.description: `raw` is always present, even on a proposal sentinel (where it explains why there is no located passage). The four parsed members are independently nullable: a locator that names a page but no table has `table: null`, not an omitted key.

applicability.description: Population applicability of the passage. Every member is independently nullable: `sex` in particular is null whenever the source does not name a single sex (D-EP3 field map — 'parsed from population when it names a sex, else null'), which is the common case, not an error.

provenance.description: Where this passage record came from. Unlike the applicability/locator fields, none of these are legitimately absent: every passage — including the minted proposal sentinel — traces to a specific RF run and source card.

evidenceId.description: The RF `extracted_points[].evidence_id` (e.g. `ev_001`), or the literal `implementation-proposal` for the minted sentinel.
